title: Linked List Cycle
description: Given a head of a linked list, determine if the linked list has a cycle in it. A linked list is said to have a cycle if there exists a node in the list that can be reached again by continuously following the next pointer. Do not use extra space for storing visited nodes.

examples.explanation: The linked list has a cycle because the tail connects back to the second node (value 2).
The linked list has a cycle because the tail connects back to the first node (value 1).
The linked list does not have a cycle because there is only one node and no connections back to it.

constraints: The number of nodes in the linked list is in the range [0, 10^4].
The values of the nodes are within the range [-10^5, 10^5].
hints: Consider using the two-pointer technique to solve the problem.
One pointer should move faster than the other to detect a cycle.
Think about how you can identify when the two pointers meet.
What happens if you encounter a null reference while traversing?
